description_long: This object is a long, straight-bladed sword with a pointed tip and a symmetrical double-edged blade. The blade is slender and tapers evenly from the hilt to the tip, suggesting it is designed for thrusting as well as cutting. The hilt features a prominent crossguard that is flat and extends horizontally, providing hand protection. The handle is cylindrical and appears to be made of the same material as the blade, ending in a faceted pommel for balance and grip security. The entire object has a uniform dark metallic finish, giving it a sleek and formidable appearance. There are no visible engravings or decorative elements, emphasizing a utilitarian and functional design.
description: A long, double-edged straight sword with a pointed tip, dark metallic finish, and a simple crossguard and pommel. The design is sleek, functional, and unadorned.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full length of the straight, double-edged blade, crossguard, and cylindrical handle with a faceted pommel.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the thin profile of the blade and the flatness of the crossguard, with minimal width visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and handle in full symmetry.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again highlights the blade's thinness and the straight alignment of the handle and pommel.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, metallic, faceted pommel, no visible grip texture
